qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution via loadFile() and load() allows attacker-controlled Object.prototype manipulation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same CVE-2026-33863, same package (convict), same vulnerable function (overlay() in main.js), same attack vectors (load()/loadFile()/schema initialization with __proto__/constructor.prototype), and same root cause (unguarded recursive merge in overlay() allows __proto__ and constructor.prototype keys to reach Object.prototype).

selected_finding.title: Prototype pollution via loadFile() and load() allows attacker-controlled Object.prototype manipulation

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The PoC was executed against convict v6.2.4 and confirmed Object.prototype pollution: after loading a malicious JSON config via config.loadFile(), freshly created empty objects {} inherited attacker-controlled properties ({}.polluted === "yes", {}.isAdmin === true, {}.polluted_array[0] === "owned"). This directly demonstrates the advisory-described vulnerability: the overlay() function in main.js recursively merges config data without checking for __proto__ keys, allowing Object.prototype pollution via load()/loadFile().
evidence: PoC cloned convict v6.2.4 and installed dependencies
malicious_config.json with __proto__ containing polluted, isAdmin, and polluted_array properties was loaded via config.loadFile()
Object.prototype was verified clean before the exploit (testObj1.polluted === undefined)

poc_executions.observed_effect: After loading malicious_config.json via config.loadFile(), Object.prototype was polluted: {}.polluted === "yes", {}.isAdmin === true, {}.polluted_array[0] === "owned". The PoC exited with code 0 confirming successful exploit.
